response.justification: While Bogdan is an exceptionally experienced and talented software engineer (19 years across diverse tech stacks), this role requires deep C++ and industrial automation expertise. His documented C++ experience is minimal (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015) and largely absent from a 10-year career trajectory focused on web, backend, and Node.js development. He lacks the industrial IoT, embedded systems, and cybersecurity background (IEC 62443) central to the role's requirements. The mismatch is fundamental—this is not a case of transferable skills, but rather a different engineering discipline entirely.
strengths: Exceptional engineering breadth and seniority with 19 years of experience across web, backend, and embedded systems; proven ability to lead teams and mentor on software craftsmanship.

Strong in modern development practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, agile) and technical architecture documentation; founded and maintains open-source projects demonstrating architectural depth.
Natural communicator and educator, evidenced by workshops, talks, and mentoring across multiple companies; demonstrates ability to distill complex concepts.
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated—only ~6 months documented at National Instruments (2014–2015), insufficient for a C++ architect role requiring STL, multi-threading, and high-performance systems design.

Career is web/backend-focused (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang); no industrial automation, IoT protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, OPC UA), embedded systems, or industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) background evident.
No Azure cloud experience (preferred), no MQTT or message-broker architecture work, and no demonstrated secure development lifecycle practices specific to industrial systems.
